fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 12-year-old female brought by mother for sadness related to chronic medical condition.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 12-year-old female with Type 1 diabetes presenting with low mood over past two months. She reports feeling frustrated and sad about having to manage her diabetes while peers do not have similar restrictions. She denies any thoughts of self-harm or suicidal ideation. Patient states she sometimes feels different from friends and embarrassed about checking blood sugar at school. She has been compliant with diabetes management but expresses resentment about the condition. Recently, a classmate made an insensitive comment about her needing to use the school nurse office regularly. Sleep and appetite are normal. She continues to enjoy swimming and maintains good friendships. Mother reports patient's diabetes control is good and medical team is pleased with management. (Nurse Practitioner Patricia Lee, PMHNP-BC)
fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Adjustment disorder with depressed mood related to chronic illness, mild.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with both parents; has Type 1 diabetes with good medical management; struggling with feeling different from peers; recent insensitive peer comment; engaged in athletics; supportive family; good diabetes control.
Assessment_and_Plan: Adjustment difficulties related to managing chronic illness in adolescence.
Protective factors: excellent diabetes management, supportive family, engaged in swimming, good peer relationships overall, no suicidal ideation.
Referral for counseling focusing on coping with chronic illness and building resilience.
Connection with peer support group for youth with Type 1 diabetes.
School consultation to address peer education and reduce stigma.
Validation of feelings and normalization of adjustment challenges.
Coordination with endocrinology team regarding psychological support resources.
Follow-up in 4 weeks to reassess mood; symptoms likely to improve with support and processing.
fields.Author: Patricia Lee, PMHNP-BC (Nurse Practitioner)
